Mtn To Leave To Take Limited Subsequent Deposition
Off Calendar Jul-29-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Motion for leave to take subsequent deposition of defendant pursuant to CCP 2025.610(b) denied for lack of good cause. Plaintiff has already examined defendant about his review of medical records in first deposition and had opportunity to fully cross examine defendant then. Plaintiff failed to show good cause why he did not obtain discovery in a sequence that would not require a second deposition of plaintiff or why he was prevented from fully examining witness at first deposition. Further, motion to amend complaint to add an additional cause of action, successfully made by plaintiff on June 20, 2005, is not good cause to take a subsequent deposition; plaintiff represented in that motion that "defendant is not prejudiced by the amendment, since the same operative set of facts are alleged, and defendant has, through discovery, been made fully aware of the additional facts which plaintiff seeks to include." Meckfessel Decl. Ex. A, page 2. Plaintiff also fails to show why other forms of discovery could not be used which would be less burdensome to defendant than a subsequent deposition. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT ORDER SPECIFICALLY CONFORMING TO THE TENTATIVE RULING. (622/MMILLER)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production; Declaration
Off Calendar Jun-20-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production is granted in part and moot in part. As to Request Nos. 1, 3-25, 27, and 30-32, Plaintiff shall respond further, verifying that no documents have been withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege with the exception of documents between Plaintiff and his counsel. As to communications or documents between Plaintiff and his counsel, they are not included in any documents which may be sought or must be produced. The failure to create a privilege log does not waive any privilege. The remainder of the motion is moot.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
